name: North Dakota (United States)
description: A blue field bearing a stylized bald eagle with outspread wings, holding an olive branch and a bundle of arrows, with the motto 'E PLURIBUS UNUM' on a streamer in its beak. Above the eagle a sunburst of thirteen yellow stars; below, a red ribbon scroll inscribed 'NORTH DAKOTA'.
history: Adopted on 3 March 1911. The design closely mirrors the regimental flag carried by the First North Dakota Volunteer Infantry during the Spanish-American War (1898) and the Philippine-American War (1899-1902); only the addition of the 'NORTH DAKOTA' scroll distinguishes it. The flag has been criticized for lacking distinctively North Dakotan symbolism but has been retained without significant change.

features.role: a bald eagle bearing on its breast a shield of thirteen red and white stripes, holding an olive branch and a bundle of arrows in its talons; in its beak a scroll reading 'E PLURIBUS UNUM'; above the eagle a fan of thirteen yellow stars rising from clouds; below, a red scroll bearing the words 'NORTH DAKOTA'
features.symbolism: the design is taken from the regimental flag carried by the First North Dakota Volunteer Infantry in the Spanish-American War and the Philippine-American War
